name: Khao Pad Sapparot (Stir Fried Rice and Pineap
recipeIngredient: 2 green parts of 2 spring onions, coarsely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the pineapple in half lengthwise and scoop out the fruit using a curved "grapefruit knife" or any knife. Chop it into bite-sized chunks.
2. In a bowl, combine the chopped pineapple, shallots, chili, ginger, scallion, and coriander. Add a pinch of salt to bring out the juice.
3. Heat about one tablespoon of oil in a wok. Stir fry the dried shrimp until crispy and the oil is aromatic. Remove with a slotted spoon and drain. Set aside.
4. Add another tablespoon of oil, and stir fry the garlic until golden brown. Add the rice and stir thoroughly.
5. Add fish sauce and sugar, and continue stirring. When the rice is heated through, add the pineapple mixture and cooked shrimp. Stir until well combined.
6. Pour the mixture into the pineapple shells, garnish with coriander leaves, and serve.
Note: If you prefer a darker-colored fried rice, replace half the fish sauce with dark sweet soy sauce.
Variation: Put the fruit mixture and stir-fried rice in separate bowls to chill all ingredients. Before serving, mix them together and place in pineapple skins. For cold dishes, add a few maraschino cherries as garnish.
This vegetarian dish is unusual for Thai cuisine but can be enjoyed with hot curries or spicy chili dishes on a hot day. Choose a medium-sized pineapple that is sweet and juicy.
